reviews.review_description:
Edit: I've knocked this down to a two as the handling is inconsistently awful. I saw many reviews complain about this but didn't initially have a problem, however, not that I've ground my way yo better tures and suspension, the handling is far worse, often undriveable. But, then again, sometimes you can have a few races where it seems fine. These off road vehicles woth huge tyres are quickly disabled and your race destroyed for merely touching some surface dust sand. Preposterous a.i. The entire thing is frustrating from so many levels. As said previously it could be amazing, but needs either more hands on de k or persons better qualified.

This game isn't terrible, it's simply o.k., the sad thing is, it could be amazing.

The game lacks heart and feels kinda empty. If they leant into the whole arcade vibe, it would be much better, think nineties Sega. Blue skies, cheezy commentary, arrows that point the way, an actual arcade mode that randomly selected six tracks. A guy or gal waving a flag at the beginning of a race.

Another huge problem is the music, it's frankly awful. The glitch hop of the main menu system is drab and makes me want to switch it off. They're going for this X Games extreme vibe with lots of metal, but again, it isn't good metal. Get some upbeat, melodic driving tunes, not just something thst you think fits the aesthetic.

Engine note is weak and uninspired. This is an off road racing game, buggy engines are raucous and ear splitting. These sound like a lawn mower. 

Unless you play woth virtual steering, which frankly I don't think anybody does, your players hands don't grip the wheel. This not only looks stupid, but is a total immersion breaker. 

On dusty tracks debris ought to be flying off your front wheels and the track,  it's as clean as a whistle


I like the customisation but the money earns us too low or the upgrades are too expensive. Either way the grind got boring really fast. 

Simple fixes:
Improve engine
Update soundtrack
Add voices commentary
Add auto hand grip toggle option. 
Add an arcade mode. 

The base model is good, is just needs overhauling and a new skin.

Could be the best racer on the system

Do you like your driving games twitchy? Like, really twitchy? Then you are gonna love this. 
Developers please, roll your sleeves up and sort out the extremely difficult and unfair driving model. This is so close to being a great racer but this needless hair trigger stuff makes you just give up in the end. I played a lot if this but in the end I just thought "nah, it's just not fair and not fun" 
Massive shame because so many elements are great. But you'll end up walking away just like me.

The game is fun I like the game the colors the graphics the steering is good only problem the game you optimize on is the cars they wait list The car bump into you are you turn a certain way your car go flying like a ride doll The cars don't have no sense of wait to it it's like they floating cars cars like they got helium in it or something so somebody bump into you and knock you afterward you turn a certain way your car go flying everywhere like I have no sense of weight or somebody crash into you your car go flying or they cargo flying it needs weight to the cars to make the game better it's when you run or crashing to somebody the cars won't go flying all over the place like they act like they in some kind of gravity field if the cars have some kind of sense of weight to it the game be more fun and more insight into play and somebody bump into you or you bump into them or something like that you turning you're not going sliding all over the place and flying that need to be optimized fixed for the game to be good they got in need weight to the cars some kind of weight thing flying over to all other places like a bouncing balloon please fix it
Nobody plays it, bugs. And not fun AT ALL.

reviews.review_title: 16th of Feb and updates much better now.
reviews.review_description: …..this game been updated and now drives smoothly and way less slide and bumps. The cars now have some weight. Thanks DEVs❤️❤️
The virtual wheel is janky and unusable. Using the stick to steer only enabled on the left stick. I hate steering with my left hand. This game could have been decent with a little polish.
Exo Cars is fun and visually immersive on Quest 3. Please add longer race tracks in future updates, as longer tracks would greatly improve immersion and replay value
But only just obviously the psvr version was more important to you.
Ok

I'm used to deliberately deceptive marketing media in the Meta store but the visual downgrade from the store video in EXOcars is pretty severe.

The person who decided to promote the Quest version with this media has no shame. The disclaimer at the bottom of the video begins with "CAPTURED IN-ENGINE" implying that it's the engine used for the game they're trying to sell. If they were honest they'd have added "BUT NOT THE ENGINE USED IN THIS QUEST VERSION."

The visuals have been mercilessly stripped to the bone to get it to run on the Quest 2. No shadows, (not even primitive "blob shadows" like they've had in racing games for decades), no headlight beams at night, no rain during storms, a substantial reduction in geometry complexity, flat lighting, etc.

EXOcars desperately needs proper Quest 3 support.

On the plus side it runs fairly smoothly and the gameplay is solid, (aside from the combat).

The cars are loosely sprung and prone to bouncing out of control, so this is not a beginner friendly racer.

After I switched to thumb stick turning and backed off the steering sensitivity it took around a half hour to get used to the handling. It's fun navigating these wild machines around the courses, but without a multiplayer community it'll get boring fairly quickly.

The courses are well designed and varied, but unfortunately most of the effort put in by the environmental artists was stripped away when porting it down to the Quest 2.

The optional combat should be left set to the default of "off".  It's terrible and feels like something that was tacked on at the last minute.

Racing bots is somewhat fun, and since multiplayer seems to be stone cold dead that's a good thing. Creating a public lobby then waiting for someone to wander in has been an exercise in futility. I wonder if multiplayer would be dead had they built the game for the Quest 3 instead of ignoring it in favor of a discontinued headset.

Despite the fun I'm having with this game I can't in good conscience give it a high score. If they ever release a proper Quest 3 update I'll raise my rating.

This is a brilliantly crafted racer that really pushes the hardware to its limits. The tracks are well-designed and varied, offering plenty of visual interest and challenges. The racing itself feels great—fast, engaging, and just difficult enough to keep you coming back. The menu design is sleek, and the soundtrack perfectly complements the game’s style.

That said, once you’ve unlocked all the levels and maxed out your car, there’s not much left to strive for. It would have been great to see features like driving-impact damage that affects performance, or fun little twists—like animals crossing the track, where hitting them could cost or earn you points, or tire-spike hazards to shake things up. Small dynamic elements like these could really bring the game world to life.

There’s also some roughness in the customisation system. While you can buy cosmetic upgrades like new colours, they either don’t appear during races or sometimes vanish completely after exiting the menu—while still charging your in-game currency. That’s a frustrating bug that definitely needs fixing.

The driving physics are another mixed bag. The handling starts off extremely slippery—almost uncontrollable at times. It does improve once your car is fully upgraded, but even then, it remains overly loose for a game that otherwise feels so polished.
Everytime I got on this game nobody was even playing online. I was expecting more online lobbies in this game.

As if this game wasn't fun enough in its original racing form....they just introduced a new combat zone.....anyone remember Unreal Tournament?....Yes.....it's Unreal Tournament in an Exocar! DUUUUDE AWESOME

reviews.review_title: A little upset and totally confused
reviews.review_description: I have to ask to developers what are you doing this game was perfect when it first came out didn't need any tweaking or anything now the speed is super slow it looks pixally and jacket on the environment if it ain't broke don't fix it otherwise you will break it as you have done I'm disappointed that this game is going backwards getting uglier and becoming worse damaged with each update can you please restore it to its original state of first release everything was perfect but now it seems like I'm playing a low budget driving game yes I said driving not racing because it's too slow now and everything seems to be ruined it was perfect during release but some people complain I guess they want the car in the living room so you make patches only to break or damage the good things about the game this was one of my favorites I played it so much now it's very hard for me to stay in tune with it because I know what it was versus what it is now and this is not the same game I feel duped like after you get all the money you lower the graphics or slow the game down and make changes to the pixels or environment that it may be a lower grade game not cool please fix it please restore it back to the natural State regardless of what most complainers complain about there was nothing wrong with this game at all I understand you want to add new features but sometimes that damages the whole experience the speed is too slow now I don't feel like I'm racing I feel like I'm on a slow driving simulation game of what could have been when it already was great I'm only giving three stars because I know what it was and what it is now until it's restored or when it's restored I would gladly not only refer others to buy it but give you those five stars would have giving you 10 if I could but after taking a small break and coming back seeing the massive changes that are not for the best I can only give three at this point seriously no disrespect just being honest please do better the VR community is depending on it

developer_response.body:
Hello! We have not released any updates that reduce speed or degrade graphics.
We added the ability to increase speed by upgrading your racing buggy, but the car should not be slower than before!
If you're experiencing this issue, please contact us on Discord or email us at info@xocus.co, and we will do our best to help you!

reviews.review_description:
I am INCREDIBLY infuriated that I WASTED money on this "driving on fu***ng ice" simulator! The controls are nearly impossible to work with and your f**#&#@ buggy slips and slides more than it ANYTHING else! Making it impossible to win any da*n thing at all! 
Even if it didn't control like a busted RC car on a melting ice river, the "racing" basically amounts to time attacks only, with other racers being nothing but ghosts of other players that don't even have collision physics (not that the game needed another feature to make it even more impossible..). So I just keep them turned off so they at least aren't blocking precious sight of these tracks that may as well all be completely iced over...

So I lost precious money that I just BARELY fu***fg have on this p.o.s. useless game..
If you don't own it yet, then don't make the same mistake I did. Go for another racing title and not waste your own money...

Me and 2 friends just bought this game, it's difficult but plays well and looks great. The only problem is we our cars are different speeds. When we leave the line my car does like 100kph the next player 70kph and the last 50kph. Hope I've explained that well, if we do a drag race I just leave them behind through no fault of their own.
If this could be fixed it would be 4 or 5 stars.
If developers are reading....
This is in private room.
It doesn't matter who is hosting, ie hoster doesn't necessarily get faster car.
I am on quest 3, they are on quest 2, I have been consistently faster, but doesn't explain the speed difference between them two.
Hope you can fix, If so i will change my review.

developer_response.body: Hello! We will definitely check this, and if necessary, we will fix it in the next update!

reviews.review_description:
You know the Cars Wii game? This is that, but in VR. I’m not a huge racing game girl, but this game had me playing way more than I expected. The controls were easy to pick up and I could jump right into playing. I even figured out drifting, which I’m yet to do in Mario Kart. 

I was nervous that I would get motion sick, but I played seated and had no issues!

I love everything about this game. Plenty of tracks, racing with or without bots, ghosts or physical karts, a progression system, and online rankings through the career. The races really are intense too with nice weather effects on some maps and even small jumps feel crazy. I wish i had more than 5 stars to give.

developer_response.body:
Hello! We continue to improve the game and will soon release a new mode for our users!
Reviews like yours give us the motivation to keep making VR games! Thank you so much for that!

reviews.review_description: Man I have nothing to say but after playing this I have to go change my star ratings and reviews on the other racing games that came out before this that I called the best because I want more people playing this not just for me but for them too lol.

reviews.review_title: A very fun racing game on standalone VR!
reviews.review_description:
It’s awesome to see a VR racing game on Meta Quest with a career mode, realistic driving mechanics, and multiplayer. 

As someone who likes racing games but isn’t exactly the best at them, it’s great to see so much content in the single player.

The driving mechanics are very smooth and intuitive. I was able to hop in and get right into the action without difficulty  

Loaded with replay value, this a great game for any racing fan!

EXOcars is a fun VR racing game that combines the thrill of off-road adventures with high-speed asphalt racing. Players can customize their vehicles, participate in tournaments with real prizes, and enjoy a variety of game modes and seasonal challenges. With its immersive gameplay and decent graphics, EXOcars offers an experience that appeals to both casual gamers and racing enthusiasts.

EXOcars is a game that truly raises the bar for VR racing! The blend of off-road and street racing keeps the excitement levels high, and the ability to customize cars adds a personal touch that makes each race feel unique. The tournaments with real prizes are a fantastic incentive for skilled racers, and the seasonal events keep the gameplay fresh and engaging. Whether you’re racing through rugged wilderness or burning rubber in parking garages, EXOcars delivers an unmatched adrenaline rush. It's a must-try for anyone looking to experience the thrill of racing in virtual reality. 

While EXOcars offers thrilling gameplay, it does come with a learning curve when it comes to steering and control. New players may find the physics challenging initially, as the game requires precise handling to navigate turns and varied terrains. The realistic dynamics can make it easy to lose control, especially at high speeds or when trying to execute maneuvers. Spinning out can happen quickly if players are not cautious, adding an extra layer of excitement and challenge or even frustration. However, this aspect also enhances the overall experience, as mastering the controls becomes a rewarding journey that keeps players engaged and eager to improve their skills.

Tip for beginners: go to the settings option in the game. Set "Control Presets" to "Low." Play career mode and upgrade the surface grip as often as you can, whenever possible. Then upgrade suspension. This will help to improve track traction.

There's a handful of racing options on Quest but EXOcars is by far the most polished and comes fully-loaded with features. It reminds me of the old-school racing games that Codemasters used to make like Colin McRae Rally or the DIRT series. 

While I'm not a hardcore racing sim fan, there's plenty to enjoy for casual players as well as hardcore gear-heads. You can customize your ride, your gloves, heck, even your helmet, and race across tons of tracks. 

It's worth mentioning just how good the tracks look as well. You'll zoom through stadiums packed with cheering fans, down dirt roads, and through urban landscapes. All of them are super detailed and are fun to race on.

There's even fun seasonal events and rallies that you can compete in, so whether you're a hardcore racing fan or just have the occasional need for speed EXOcars is your best bet on Quest.

There are a ton of different options from maps to car customization that makes it easy to jump back into. There is a bit of a learning curve / adjustment needed for people who aren't used to racing simulations. The career mode was the highlight for me, as upgrading the car (not customizing it) really led to better driving, and the progression felt good. Would recommend for people who are looking for a good racing sim that isn't overwhelming

This is a pretty good go cart racer that takes a bit of practice to get the hang of. It's got a decent variety of tracks and you can upgrade your cart as you progress. I like the idea of competing against other player's ghosts instead of AI bots. Can get crazy on the dirt tracks and feels a little like the Dukes of Hazzard.

Exocars is a pretty action-packed VR racing game, really filling a very much-needed hole in the library of Quest. It combines solo time trials with chaotic multiplayer modes and vehicular combat for a number of different thrilling experiences. Solo mode is really great for simply focusing on bettering times on leaderboards, while multiplayer shines when you can find a full lobby-albeit, at times sparse player activity may make this tough. With a variety of maps, a set of weapons, and over-the-top physics, the game is a blast-whether one is racing against the AI or others. Detailed graphics and immersive driving physics add to its appeal, though occasional bugs in multiplayer can disrupt gameplay. A few rough edges don't hold Exocars back from being a fun, immersive VR racing experience. With innovative features and high replayability, this is definitely a hidden gem no VR racing fan should miss.

I do deepsea fishing, I scuba, skydive, MotoX (In real live, not games, I can send vids of all of these activities. Like I have over 200 skydives (freefly (like upside down, tumble. Spirals, not even formation) and yes have played many VR games on my Quest 3s. All types, this game is one of those, This game made me nauseous in 3 minutes. Horrible. Looks so so great, the cars and environments. BUT drives like you're on oily butter road or ice?? Why?? as it floats around from side to side. I literally could not dial in my senses because of how the vehicle behave. Guaranteed nausea (as I'm writing this still feel green lol) I say all the above to let you know how bad something has to be to get me to feel like this. Thought I am immune to motion sickness since have not experienced since got VR. Congrats Exo you did it. I am going to try and get my money back but probably cant since I'm in South Africa. But this review would maybe help others decide. If you even slightly proned to motion sickness you WILL be in a really bad state even trying this. To The developers : you have this 90% there guys. Lock down the movement of the vehicles. Go actual go karting or rally with dune buggies... They dont behave like this. If you can dial in handling and momentum movement you'll have a winner. For now.... Its really really bad aspect of the game. Hoping you do since I would love a working product. PS please don't excuse this with one can get motion sickness or some can't pkay VR. As I said I have never even felt motions sickness before. It's not me its the game. I read a review where you excused the bad handling with, you don't want to make it to arcade or such. C'mon guys, so many complaints about the handling. My friends and family members now tried it on one of my Quest 3 headsets and they also completely agreed. Please revisit yhe handling in the game. Stuff don't move lile this lol.

developer_response.body:
Good day!

We’ve released a big update!
We tried to take your feedback and suggestions into account—even went go-karting ourselves to feel the difference! 😃
We’ve added tuning to the game, so now you can adjust your buggy’s grip on the surface before each race! We’ve also introduced new tracks and game modes!
Please try out the new gameplay experience and let us know what you think! We’d love it if you updated your rating for the better!
If you need to unlock certain in-game content, such as tuning or tracks faster, feel free to email us at info@xocus.co, and we’ll be happy to help!
Our goal is to create an awesome game, and your opinion is very important to us! 🚀

reviews.review_title: Great racing game for quest standalone
reviews.review_description:
There are a lot of great racing games running on PCVR, but the quest standalone store really lacked titles for this genre, exocars offers a very nice racing experience, I wouldn't count on playing multiplayer very much as it was almost impossible for me to find a public lobby each time I tried, but if the playerbase grows then your experience might be different. Playing solo is already good enough, and you can try to beat other player'a best times.
It can be pretty motion sickness inducing, so I recommend to start with the most comfortable settings and if there's no issue remove them step by step. I also realized from other racing games that having airflow in your room helps against the motion sickness, either having a fan on or opening a window can help.

If you like driving games, and if you like VR. This is the game you are looking for right here. The controls for driving is as close as it can get to the level of using a force feedback wheel without the force feedback ofc, but the feeling of driving stays the same. You can also drift quite easily and feels realistic and fun. If anything I prefer this mode of controls because I don't need to setup my wheel everytime when I want to play. And the addition of multiplayer just makes the game go from a 10/10 time trial racing game to a 100/10. Having the ability to create a lobby and invite your friends to prove who truly is the best is incredible, or even join random public lobby's are fun to race new people to improve my skills. Overall this game is more than great: the upgrading, the driving, the drifting, the leader board, the events, the multiplayer; it's all done well in VR making the driving as interactable as possible and you can tell this is just the start of what they can do with this and I can't wait to see what that is.

reviews.review_title: One of the best racing games I’ve played
reviews.review_description: This game is honestly one of the best games I’ve played not even just in the racing category and I didn’t want to stop playing. This game is fantastic because it has a ton of different items to use on you’re enemy’s or yourself to help you win, kind of like Mario cart, and the different tracks/maps are really well designed and very fun. This game always made me want to come back and keep playing because it was just so great, though it does have some bugs that sometimes are a little trouble like how sometimes only the host will, be able to see the other players and you have to reset the lobby to fix it which makes you want to quit sometimes, but also the single player doesn’t have any bugs that I found, and it is also really fun because you can earn money so you can upgrade your car to do better in races. All in all this game is great for anyone who loves racing games like Mario cart and like to test their skills.

I very much enjoyed my time with EXOcars. Great set pieces including towering redwoods and a coastal swept lighthouse offer a fantastic sense of scale and a strong presence in this virtual world. 
Numerous modes including Career, Mupliplayer, a holiday event and weapon based matches help round out the track and provide numerous avenues to express your inner race demon. I'm hoping the developer is able to 
incorporate a custom playlist for music and collision/damage modeling to pair with the already challenging yet superb racing physics. If you've been itching to get into the driver seat then give this title a spin. You won't be disappointed!

The learning curve for controls are fairly steep, but there are plenty of settings and once you get locked in, it is super satisfying! This will require more patience and skill than a mario kart-style racer, and it has a more dirt rally vibe. 

You’ll need strong vr legs, but if you’re looking for a less arcadey racer on quest, this is it.

Exo cars is a polished racing game which can be played as a straight racer or as a combat racer (in multiplayer only currently) e.g. think Mario Kart with rockets and turbo boosts.

There are 9 unique track with variants (different times/weather condition's) bringing it to a grand total of 27 tracks (at the time of writing

Controls are solid, with both VR wheel controls or stick controls for turning.
Physics of the car also make them fun to drive.

The meat and bones of the game is the career mode where you gradually unlock all the tracks as well as currency.
The currency is used to improve your cars stats as well as for customization option (ready to take online and to compete with other players)

The dev has also had (at the time of writing) special events.
With an Exo Champ tournament where you can win actual prizes for doing well.
As well as the Winter Event which is based off the training track. 
On this map you race around to compete to be the fastest to collect presents dotted around the map.

Who knows what other special events will be rotated into the modes line up over time?

I would love to see bigger multiplayer lobbies in the future (currently support max 4 players) 
Perhaps some more seasonal events or new tracks to be added over time.
But as it stands the game is a fun game to play both solo or with others.

It's alright.  Physics are a definitely floaty and something is off that will get you VR sick especially for newcomers.  Wheel with hands is not good, joystick is ok.  Given the limited racing games on Quest its worth considering, but content is also pretty limited with the same levels just recycled with different daytime lighting.

This game is fantastic. It feels like a solid well put together experience that I would definitely share with friends.

Would love to see a ranked mode & bigger lobbies eventually!

Very simple and easy to learn controls, easy visuals to go along with it so practically anyone can pick up this game. Definitely a 5/5 for controls

Graphics are solid, levels are large, well optimized and a solid experience for virtual reality. Smooth frames, absolutely solid U.I. System. Can get a huge selection of customizable options for your car. Great single player leader board system. 5/5 graphics and gameplay. (It felt like mario kart and twisted metal withe better graphics.)

There really is a bunch of content to explore and try to unlock through racing but i feel after the 3rd race it can get stale. Theres not a story or anything to make me feel emotionally attached to the single player other than trying to unlock new levels. There were quite a bit of bugs with connecting in multiplayer but when it did connect it was fantastic. Would definitely recommend great game. 4.5/5 over all

Potentially a good racing game. Fun with friends in multiplayer. There is variety in the maps and several choices for visual modification of the car. But at the moment have been stuck in the environment a lot and the control of the car is very slippery without any upgrades. But good potential if they adds more car upgrades, more maps and events in the future.

This is, to my awareness, the only VR stand-alone rally game. It has lots of tracks, consistent updates, customization, and I think wheel support (although I don’t have one)
I appreciate the amount of tracks, the music is well produced, the ghost players you go against in singe player is well matched, the cars look nice and a lot of things are well polished.

However, there are still some problems, in no particular order.
The music, although good, changes genre constantly. The game opens with a hard rock small snippet, and then cuts to the main menu music which is like, Lofi trap, and then back to rock in the levels.
The handbrake I feel moves far too little, and it’s not adjustable in any way. Small problem but gets to me idk why.
The polishing is.. selective? Inconsistent? The main menu is well thought out and the adjusting of the car is very nice but then you go to the level sub menus and the background is completely black and feels lower quality. And then the ghosts are pleasing and work decent enough. But pressing restart, just sends you back to the menu? Like come on, the restart makes me especially angry because I mess up a  and it makes the game a lot more frustrating, it’s just the little things sometimes.
The driving also is rather frustrating sometimes, it adds up quickly when you mess up, there’s no padenotes, no minimap, and hairpin turns at  tree r a long straight. I like being at least top 3 and sometimes missing a turn repeatedly and having to take 10 seconds per restart doesn’t help.

Overall, 4/5, fun game when you do good, only one of its kind, maybe just a skill issue but it’s very frustrating.

The stunning tracks, immersive VR visuals, and responsive controls make every race unforgettable. Customizing cars and unlocking upgrades add depth, while multiplayer mode keeps the adrenaline pumping. Smooth performance and dynamic details make this a standout experience. A must-play for VR racing fans!

This game has all the classic style rally racing basics. From various track styles to custom tuning of the major components. It’s not as complex as many PC/Console games, certainly slimmed down for the Quest, but not too bad. It has a straightforward campaign where you race every track at least once, which is a few locations at various times of the day and slight variations to the tracks. All while making it look like you are competing against ghost players and trying to beat the specific track times to get more money for upgrades and customizations. Just, user beware, don’t try to rush after a race and click the buttons till it runs its totals, otherwise you may not accumulate the XP and money.

There is a fair bit of customization, however it is all behind the currency that you earn. There aren’t even any basic options to mix it up for new players. So, if you do end up playing a multiplayer game, you know clearly who just started out.

Speaking of multiplayer, it still needs a lot of work. Lots of various issues with it and it’s not very rewarding. It’s fun playing with other people in various modes, but besides the points you build up for position in each game, which are only for that room, you get nothing. They have a combat mode too, where you can play kind of like those other multiplayer “kart” games.

Overall it is a nice game and you can see from recent updates that they are clearly taking feedback to make improvements. I hope they keep up with it and continue improving it because this could be THE rallycross game of Quest.

If you are looking for a racing game for you and some friends, this might be the right choice. Private lobbies are quickly done and it's easy to join. We had the most fun with active battle mode, which is like Mario Kart with e.g. rockets, mines, and such.

The controls are tricky and you have to go into the control settings. The default position and e.g. turn speed are most likely not fitting for you. Once I changed some of them, the handling became better.

You also need to play Singleplayer/Career mode to earn money. Don't use it for customizations though. Before a race starts you have the upgrades on the right side. I've seen a review that said that there is no tuning... that's not true. I upgraded grip and suspension, and it is noticeable! So keep that in mind when you play. The learning curve is pretty steep compared to other racing games.

Seems to get regular updates and special (seasonal) events like tournaments for real prices or a Winter/Xmas event.

EXOcars is so close to the arcade racing game I’ve been craving in VR. The graphics are decent enough to be immersive when traveling down the road at high speeds, there is a wide variety of tracks, both stick turning and motion controls (thank you!), upgrades to fine tune your car, multiplayer races, and even a “Mario Kart” mode with powerups. This is quite the package, but there are a few problems that take the experience down from 5 stars.

First and most egregious is the handling at the start of the game. To put it bluntly, you are driving on ice. Now, through completing races and beating time trials, you earn currency to upgrade your car. Do yourself a favor and get the first two or three handling upgrades before anything else. At this point, the racing actually becomes really fun, but you have to get through the painful early game where you will constantly spin out. I was able to get the required upgrades within three or four races, so it is not a huge time commitment, but honestly the players should not be expected to earn the ability to effectively play the game.

The only other issues I have are minor ones. There are a few too many obstacles on some courses, and there is this annoying fog on a few tracks that make them look ugly. I would have also liked to see a more in-depth career mode. Currently, all we have is a series of races with medals awarded for beating certain times.

If you are like me and have been wanting a solid arcade racer on Quest, I think EXOcars is worth a pickup. I haven’t found anything else that scratches that high speed, arcady/drifty racing experience. Be aware, you NEED to get those handling upgrades before you will have a good time, but once you’ve got those you are golden.

ExoCars VR is an absolute blast to play! The graphics are stunning, and the gameplay feels so immersive. The controls are intuitive, and you can really feel the car’s weight and physics, which makes every race feel authentic. I love the variety of tracks available, and I can’t wait to see more added in the future. The sound design is top-notch, and the music adds so much energy to the races. Adding more customization options for cars and a broader selection of tracks would make it even better, but it’s already an incredible experience. Highly recommend this for any VR racing fan!

This VR experience is a thrilling racing game that truly captures the excitement and adrenaline rush of off-road racing. I was eager to dive into it, and it certainly did not disappoint.

Upon entering the game's vibrant world, you're greeted with stunning landscapes ranging from bustling streets to serene mountains and tropical paradises. The track design is commendable, with a perfect blend of ramps, drifts, and natural obstacles that keep you on your toes. The attention to detail in landscape variety adds to the immersion, making each race feel fresh and challenging.

One of the standouts is the graphics and physics. The crisp visuals paired with a rock-solid frame rate make for an extremely smooth experience. The realistic car physics enhance this even further; I could genuinely feel the suspension working over rough terrains, adding a layer of authenticity that many racing games lack.

The multiplayer aspect is another highlight. Competing against ghosts of real players adds a competitive edge that pushes you to constantly improve your skills. Real-time multiplayer races are where the fun truly peaks—there’s nothing quite like outmaneuvering your friends or meeting new rivals in social lobbies.

 the game offers an exhilarating VR racing experience that's hard to match. Its combination of stellar graphics, authentic physics, and engaging multiplayer makes it a must-try for any VR enthusiast. Despite minor matchmaking issues, its overall impact left me very impressed.

Finally a buggy racing game 

I've been waiting for a buggy racing game for a while and this game really caught my eye it was like love at first sight.

Game them 10/10

This game takes me back to when I was younger and there are these old arcade buggy racing games that involves fighting just like this game the fact u can do regular racing or fighting it's amazing and now it's like playing the arcade game in vr

Coastomize 9.5/10

Ther is a pretty good amount of stuff u can get for Coastomize like your gloves,helmet,buggy rails, and the casing.

Controls 9.5/10

One thing that is cool is that u can use a acull wheel and rig but only certain stuff can be used but they got all of that on the discord server to what's compatible and not. Using the wheel in game is pretty smooth and not broken like some games can be or u can just use the joy sticks on your Controls. I recommend that for new players

And one Last thing to level up your buggy u have to play campaign but that's also pretty fun so don't worry about getting bored of grinding for money.

reviews.review_title: Great game. Needs more single player.
reviews.review_description:
This is an early review because I want to get my initial thoughts down. I'm really enjoying this so far, after about an hour of play. The graphics are great, except for one course, but I haven't played them all. The Winter Challenge was unexpectedly fun.

I used to love Rally Cross on the Xbox. It wasn't a realistic, technical rally race. Instead of was a crazy bump and bounce fest with over-the-top physics that regardless felt very right to me. I haven't had this much fun in a rally game since then. This game isn't as over the top, but that would be too much in VR. So far, this game is "just right".

I expected to not like racing against ghosts of real people. I was wrong. Instead I like having opponents with real names (and countries) to spur me on.

I expected to not like that they were ghosts and there were no car collisions. I was wrong. The courses provide enough obstacles and my results are my own. I failed because I misjudged the turn, not because "VR-obsessed" side-swiped me.

I'll update this later, after I've played more. It might end up being a 5. My only real complaint is that ghost opponents should be a mode, probably even the default mode, but I do think they should have a seperate mode with physical, touchable opponents for when you want to be truly crazy.

Update: I've played the courses and with few exceptions, they are very good. Even the one that isn't visually much to look at (forest) is still fun to play. The campaign/career is really basic, but it gets the job done for now. Once I finish that I might be done since I don't really do multiplayer. It's a good game, so I'll bring it back up from time to time, but it doesn't quite make a 5 with such a basic career mode.

EXOcars is a blast to play. The cars look great and handle well as long as you keep your hands out in front of you enough to keep tracking. Be warned though if you’re prone to motion sickness this one may get to you as you are bouncing around, jumping, and spinning around corners. If you can get past that though I definitely recommend.

For a standalone Quest game, this is quite impressive. The controls are intuitive and easy to get the hang of, and the graphics are pretty good. Overall this is a great, fun racing game that really makes you feel like you're in the middle of the action.

I like this game a lot. Theres a good skill ceiling. Feels like motorstorm and trackmania in one. Things I would like to see tires turn animations when throttling would be nice more customizable parts or colors and maybe one day custom maps or something there’s a lot of potential in this game. Feels well and the graphics and maps are really good. Theres a plane and train that go by that brings the maps alive. Definitely you should get this game it’s an easy sit down racer.￼

I struggled a bit at first with the virtual wheel after spending so much time using a physical one, but after a while, I somewhat got the hang of it. At times the game can get a tiny bit frustrating but the fun it gives more than makes up for it. I hope one day they make it cross-buy so I can replay everything on PCVR with my wheel as well as on Quest to play on the go! Multiplayer is enjoyable, haven't had any bad experiences in it, and I had fun coming up with a strategy in the Christmas event level. The campaign is nice and long and took me quite a while to get through, focusing on customizing the look of my buggy before actually upgrading things internally did cause me to struggle a bit here and there, however. ^v^

I love racing games, and I love playing them in VR. This game is great for racing fans who want a fun racing game. The tracks are nicely designed, the music makes the races feel exhilarating and the customization lets you personalize yourself and your car with a ton of options. This game is great and I recommend it to anyone wanting a fun racing game to play.

I think the game looks pretty good. The physical car handling also works well. Unfortunately I could only find two comfort options, vignette and v-helmet. They weren't enough for me to feel comfortable while playing. Got dizzy and couldn't play more.

My family would go ride dune buggies in the desert when I was young and the realism brought back so many memories. (I appreciated not having sand in my face!) the various environments were detailed and pleasing. At first, I was challenged by the skill-based sensitivity until I found that I could adjust that in settings and increase it as I improve. 

This is a great game. I played it solo and am looking forward to playing with friends.

This game rocks. I feel myself pull outta my seat when I go over the jumps. I recommend using the joystick for steering. Once you get the nuances & upgrade your vehicle a bit, it gets more & more fun. Looking forward to more track locations. 🤘

The graphics are very nice. But the physics are buggy, I just want to puke. Because there is ice instead of rubber. And for some reason in different places on the map this the graphics are very nice. But the physics are buggy, I just want to puke. Because there is ice instead of rubber. And for some reason in different places on the map this ice starts to slide even more and then less. Although you can't see anything with your eyes. I hope they fix this, although it is unlikely. to slide even more and then less. Although you can't see anything with your eyes. I hope they fix this, although it is unlikely.

developer_response.body: Hi! The physics might feel uncomfortable because your buggy hasn’t been upgraded yet. You can upgrade your car and customize it to suit your preferences! We hope you enjoy the tuning options, and if you like the game, we’d greatly appreciate it if you could update your review. Thank you!

reviews.review_description:
This game is amazing. The handling of the cars was quite smooth and I quite like being able to switch between controllers and physical controls. The variety of tracks made sure that it wasn’t too repetitive and the upgrades gave the handling a new feeling depending on how you set it up. I personally liked going into sandbox and just drifting around and doing stunts. I did encounter a few bugs that were annoying. One of them happened if you were going too fast and crashed into a wall you would get glitched into it slightly and not be able to get out about one 20th of the time. I don’t know if anyone else experienced it or if my game is just broken.
But overall, I would rate it 8.5/10

reviews.review_title: The only VR game that makes me sick
reviews.review_description:
It looked cute, and I had high hopes but the game simply isn’t thought out enough for me to play for more than five minutes. I get stuck on the very first obstacle and cant get out of the ditch.

It also makes me sick for some reason which is odd since it’s the only game that does that.

developer_response.body: Hello, we’re sorry to hear that you experienced discomfort. Unfortunately, this can sometimes happen in VR games. You can enable the headset visibility and vignette options in the settings to help reduce nausea. As for a refund, unfortunately, we don’t handle that directly - it is managed by Meta. Thank you for your understanding!

reviews.review_description:
Exocars has alot of content to offer which is great for the price. There’s are tons of maps with different variations which keeps gameplay exciting and leaderboards make you want to replay maps to climb the 
leaderboards. Customisation is also very good and it’s nice to see customisation for both the car and the player model. The competitions are a cool touch however I can’t tell if they are just for Christmas or if they will be a permanent feature. Mechanics feel ok from a driving perspective however grip on dusty or dirt maps is very weak but the buggy’s tires have huge treads? Would be the only thing I would like to see revamped but apart from that the game is great.

Exocars is my favorite standalone driving game on the quest so far. The game runs very smooth and the environments are really impressive. You get multiple steering and handbrake combinations to try out. The vehicles look really cool and offer a really unique viewing experience while driving. The multiplayer modes are great as well. There is a battle mode that is particularly thrilling. It is obvious that the developers of this game have a deep respect for the racing genre.

Exocars is an excellent car game that reminds me of trackmania (without the stunts). There's a wide range of environments to choose from, with different driving styles depending on the surface. for example, circuits such as parking lots or harbours have very sharp turns, while terrains such as forests or dirt are more prone to drifting. the game lets you choose between two driving styles: a more realistic one with your hands on the wheel, and another with the joystick (I think the joystick style is easier). Either way, driving is really fun, with very good vehicles physic.
Racing allows you to earn points which you can use to customize your vehicle with new wheels, new colors, new gloves etc...or to improve your engine, brakes etc... In the career mode (which I played the most), you race against ghosts of other players of your level, which really allows you to feel the progression without frustration.
I hope that the game will receive even more content in the future, because I think it's going to stay in my headset for a long time.

Exocars is having your own personal dune buggy anytime you want . this game is so much fun to me it gives me a bit of old school vibes racing with the new school graphics and upgrades and updates .I like that it’s single player or multiplayer so you can play alone or with friends or anyone the choice is really up to you . I like that you can customize the car which is great because the possibilities are endless . That fact that it has challenges and tournaments is another reason why I love this game . I like that there are different tracks from the streets to the desert there is something for everyone the background music is just right. this game is fun for the whole family .

reviews.review_title: I want a refund it's trash
reviews.review_description:
On Quest 3. Looks like beta, steers like alpha. The point of VR is to grab the wheel, not use thumb stick steering. Unfortunately, motion controller steering is VERY BAD in ONLY THIS GAME. I have about 10 years in daily VR experience, this was a huge disappointment. 
And the graphics OMG LOL. The cart itself does not wiggle a single bit, like your head is just static to the un-cushioned seat to the vehicle without any apparent shocks or springs. After 3 updates since launch this isn't a good VR experience, feels like a cheap 2D port.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your feedback!

We value player opinions and are continuously working to develop and improve the game!

Here are a few tips to improve your steering experience:

You can adjust the steering sensitivity in the settings.
There’s an option to use the joystick for steering, which might feel more comfortable.
On December 12, we’ll release an update introducing car upgrades, including better wheels for improved grip, making driving even smoother and more enjoyable!
We hope these tips enhance your VR experience! If they do, we’d be grateful if you could update your review. 😊

reviews.review_title: runs so smooth even on quest 2
reviews.review_description: i was shocked by the game physics when i first installed this game, and they are amazingly realistic for a game like itself. my only complaint is that is a bit on  the pricey end compared to similar rivals like formula X. Other than that, i couldn’t find anything wrong with it

This game nails what matters: cars that are fun to drive. The physics are really good, and it's fun to feel yourself getting better. The graphics are solid and there are a good amount of course options. The sound isn't mind blowing, but Exocars plays very nicely with the Quest's multitasking feature so you can put on your own music or podcasts while you cruise. Bottom line, this is the best driving experience I've had on Quest, and I'm excited to see how it develops!

We only buy Comforable games as we have motion sickness problems, we rely on the game makers to be honest! This game is NOT mark properly at all!!  This game should NOT be marketed comforable!!! It is more of a moderate on the high side! A waste of money in our house! Please developers be honest with you comfort ratings!!! Just because you want to be open to more people buying it you keep it at a comfortable rating?? We look at them for a reason! Now stuck with a game we will never play.

developer_response.body: Hi! We recently released an update that fixed various issues and bugs and introduced buggy tuning, allowing you to make driving and handling more comfortable. We hope you enjoy the update and that it improves your experience with the game. If you like the update, we’d be grateful if you could update your review ❤️

reviews.review_description: ExoCars is a simple but charming racing game. It pretty much just throws you right in, but the controls are simple and you get the hang of it pretty quickly. I appreciate the variety of maps and how each map has unique challenges such as cliffs, obstacles, and jumps. I'm not sure if I was imagining it or not but it even seemed like the terrain types handled differently which was a nice touch. I really liked that in the career mode you were driving against other players past runs instead of just boring AI. I look forward to seeing future updates!

Exocars is, without a doubt, the best racing game I've played on my Quest 3 standalone. The sense of speed is incredible and the virtual steering wheel, although it takes practice, adds a level of immersion that very few games achieve. The soundtrack is perfect to accompany the action and completes the experience.

The graphics are simply stunning. The clarity and smoothness are exceptional, and some scenarios are so detailed that it feels like you're really there. The great optimization work to achieve this level of visual quality on a standalone device like the Quest 3 is evident.

The variety of circuits is another plus, from steep mountains to urban garages. The game offers a unique and addictive driving experience.

If you are a fan of racing, this game is a must-buy. 100% recommended.

Racing a buggy with robust physics simulation is even more fun than it sounds. Color your buggy to your taste and take it for a spin on a variety of different locations. Racing against three ghost opponents in career mode, without collision, luckily, as crashing into opponents when trying to get a gold time could be frustrating. With time of day differences and weather effects the tracks show a great variety and makes racing around a blast. Drifting around narrow bends trying to stay ahead of the competition, the rough tracks with bumps and jumps will from time to time make you careen off the course. 

With multiplayer and tournament support already in place, and hopefully even more tracks to come, this racer could grow to become even better. As of right now, it is great racer with a good selection of tracks. The sense of scale in the stadium track especially is immense. The sound design does betray what I presume is a not-AAA budget, but the developers have nailed the most important aspect. A fun racer that plays great on the headset. 

Now to try and get those last gold times.

reviews.review_title: Finally a good casual VR racing game
reviews.review_description: This is the first Quest game I have been excited for in a while. Really happy to be able to experience being behind the wheel in VR. The career mode is easy to hop into and has a long list of tracks, each with their own feel, literally. You can earn in-game credit to customize/skin your vehicle and driver. I look forward to what the future holds for this game!

reviews.review_title: great racer but hard to master
reviews.review_description: This is a realistic racing game with traction and spin outs don't hop in thinking it'll be like a party game. the first few races are hard while figuring out controls and you spin out a lot but once you get better it's a lot of fun. I mainly just went for better times but multiplayer does exist and they even have tournaments in game already. there's also cosmetics you unlock but it's just different paint jobs no different car styles. overall it's just a really fun racing game that takes a little bit of time to get into it

reviews.review_title: Great game. Really tough but fair.
reviews.review_description: Exocars is a great racing game that is easy to jump into but tough to master. Driving feels great and the physics make every turn successful turn super satisfying. But make no mistake about it, this game is tough. My first runs were filled with crashing into walls but each time felt fair which is what made each successful turn feel so good. The game has been hosting tournaments for real prizes which is a cool feature as well. I look forward to improving and hopefully jumping into some more multiplayer matches in the future.
sank about 15hrs into this so far. good progress system to making cars handle better etc. graphics are excellent with no frame drops (quest 3). really good sense of speed.

If you are into driving cars and racing this is the game for you it's pretty enjoyable with a lot of tracks to drive in and great  replay ability due to always being able to beat hour time and get better at the track, the online modes are also pretty nice and enjoyable

reviews.review_title: Best Arcade Racer on the Quest
reviews.review_description:
This is the racing game you've been waiting for. A good solid arcade racer that isn't too complicated to get into, has great tracks and multiplayer. Very simple to just pick up and play and while away an afternoon or evening trying to improve track times. Reminds me a little of the Burnout series. Has decent graphics and plenty of tracks to keep you coming back. It's a 5 star experience and one of my favourite Quest games with only one concern really, and more of a plea to the Devs (who are very actively involved in making it better).

I just hope that over time the changes they make are subtle and not too drastic. The choice to add upgrades recently was good overall as they're not overly complicated. But this does immediately stop it from being a pick up and play arcade racer and has changed it to a 'who has unlocked everything racer' before new players can start realistically challenging for decent times. Personally I feel they would have been best keeping upgrades as a separate mode, or just having an arcade mode without them. 

However all that said, it's still such fun, and well worth the price.

reviews.review_title: A great game from the start
reviews.review_description:
In my opinion, as an avid racing game enthusiast, Xocus has undoubtedly surpassed expectations with their latest creation, EXO cars. Drawing inspiration from the iterative development of Omega Pilot and the refined handling, the game delivers an exceptionally realistic driving experience. The physics simulation is particularly noteworthy, although I was somewhat surprised to encounter a degree of virtual reality motion queasiness. This, however, serves as a testament to the game’s exceptional ability to replicate the dynamics of an EXO car.

Personally, I prefer to play racing games without music, and I was thoroughly impressed by the ambient sound design.

The only minor issue I have encountered so far is that after approximately an hour of gameplay on the Meta Quest 3, my headset began to experience slight overheating. While this could be attributed to hardware limitations, I believe it is important to raise awareness of this potential problem.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your feedback! We’re thrilled that you enjoyed EXOcars!

reviews.review_description: Such a blast to race these dune buggy style cars.  The tracks are varied and have weather effects.  I like the steering wheel arm adjustments so I could hold my arms in a comfortable placement.  Multiplayer felt seemless to play.  I have had a lot of fun getting better at racing faster.  I definitely feel like I am racing a buggy style vehicle.  The vehicle feedback and movement plus all the track variety make this my favorite VR racing sim.  I have played two others which I felt were good games too but ExoCars jumped to top spot for me.  Highly recommend, two thumbs up!

The game is pretty physics based, so don't expect need for speed type racing. The game is pretty fun with your friends and isn't just: go fast=win. You have to think on some parts about your speed and have to learn the handling of the vehicle. There's also a "Mario Kart" type mode where you get some power ups, it's really fun with friends. The Singleplayer is also quite fun. So far I've not had any completely game breaking bugs and had a good time with friends. Can only recommend!

reviews.review_title: Great game that needs some tweaks
reviews.review_description: Really enjoying this,the graphics look great on quest 3 and run smooth.the handling is great if you tweak some settings,the best settings are to set steering angle to 100,stick rotation to 75 and steering sensitivity to 50.the only big problem i have is with the sound,the cars sound weak and definitely need beefing up,and the race music is poor.for £15 this is worth it and looking forward to whats added in future

reviews.review_title: Fun but no instructions or explanations
reviews.review_description: This game is really fun though the cars feel like they are running on slick tires no matter the course so you gotta get good at managing drift and steering correction. I wish the game had a training course or an explanation guide somewhere beyond just showing you what your controllers do when you pause. for example, the jumping icon on your dash, does that mean if I hit a jump do I reduce my lap time or something, I don't really even have time to look at my instruments. What do the green circles mean, I assume it's optimal grip width but it doesn't line up with where my hands grip the steering wheel?! Also all the upgrades for your buggy seem to be purely cosmetic, I wish I could improve my cart, especially tire grip, though that would make multi-player unbalanced possibly.

I was hesitant because of some of the negative feedback. Bought it and was pleasantly surprised at how solid it is. First decent racing game for standalone imo. With a few improvements I'll give it a 5. Is it possible to beef up the engine sounds to something more fiery/raspy on an update? That w
ould make all the difference:) Solid game though!
I had never played a driving game before and this was a great first experience. Once you figure out the angle for the drift you feel like a super hero. Fun with friends as well!

Pretty realistic racing game its fun to drift around corners and spack into players and it has alot of customization and many maps
The campaign us fun and beating high scores is better and playing multi-player like bumper cars are hilarious will play again
